summary: Identified a viable non-portal direct capture implementation path using ext-image-copy-capture-v1 against a generated Hyprland wl_output. Live probes created, configured, discovered, captured via grim for targetability, and removed the node-scoped output. The path can provide size, DRM format/modifier constraints, presentation timestamp, and implicit/protocol-ready sync mode, but not an explicit sync fd.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Identifies candidate direct capture APIs or tools available on the reference Linux host.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: tools-and-protocols.md records local tools and active Wayland protocol globals.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Runs a bounded probe against a node-scoped madobe output where possible.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: hyprland-monitors-with-output.json, direct-grim-ppm-capture.txt, wayland-output-generated-source.txt, and cleanup snapshots.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Records whether size, format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata are available.
If viable, documents the exact implementation path for m2-capture-one-frame.
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-direct-capture-api-spike and reports/qd/m2-direct-capture-api-spike.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Node-scoped evidence and report directories only.

commandsRun.command: wayland-info -i wl_output filtered to node output
focused JSON parse, redaction scan, and line limit checks

realWorldValidation.evidence: Live Hyprland and Wayland probes were run against the reference Linux user session with cleanup evidence.

residualRisks: No ext-image-copy-capture client was implemented in this spike, so no real DMA-BUF allocation or frame-ready event was captured.
Explicit sync-fd metadata is not available from ext-image-copy-capture-v1.
Hyprland's ext-image-copy-capture implementation may reject a specific client-allocated DMA-BUF format/modifier choice at runtime; m2-capture-one-frame must handle buffer_constraints failures.
The protocol is staging and may change in future Wayland protocol releases.
